# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.224.0/http/server.ts";

type Json = null | boolean | number | string | Json[] | { [k: string]: Json };

type User = {
  id: string;
  createdAt: number;
  updatedAt: number;
  name?: string;
  email?: string;
  tags?: string[];
  attrs?: Record<string, string>;
};

type UserIndexValue = {
  userId: string;
  updatedAt: number;
};

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

const ALLOWED_FILTERS = new Set([
  "email",
  "name",
  "tag",
  "attr",
]);

const MAX_LIMIT = 200;

function json(data: Json, init: ResponseInit = {}): Response {
  const headers = new Headers(init.headers);
  headers.set("content-type", "application/json; charset=utf-8");
  return new Response(JSON.stringify(data), { ...init, headers });
}

function badRequest(message: string, details?: Json): Response {
  return json({ error: "bad_request", message, details: details ?? null }, { status: 400 });
}

function notFound(message: string): Response {
  return json({ error: "not_found", message }, { status: 404 });
}

function parseLimit(sp: URLSearchParams): number {
  const raw = sp.get("limit");
  if (!raw) return 50;
  const n = Number(raw);
  if (!Number.isFinite(n) || n <= 0) return 50;
  return Math.min(Math.floor(n), MAX_LIMIT);
}

function parseCursor(sp: URLSearchParams): string | null {
  const c = sp.get("cursor");
  if (!c) return null;
  try {
    atob(c);
    return c;
  } catch {
    return null;
  }
}

function encodeCursor(key: Deno.KvKey): string {
  return btoa(JSON.stringify(key));
}

function decodeCursor(cursor: string): Deno.KvKey | null {
  try {
    const txt = atob(cursor);
    const parsed = JSON.parse(txt);
    if (Array.isArray(parsed)) return parsed as Deno.KvKey;
    return null;
  } catch {
    return null;
  }
}

function nowMs(): number {
  return Date.now();
}

function normalizeStr(s: string): string {
  return s.trim();
}

function assertUserId(userId: string): string | null {
  const id = normalizeStr(userId);
  if (!id) return null;
  if (id.length > 200) return null;
  return id;
}

function userKey(userId: string): Deno.KvKey {
  return ["users", userId] as const;
}

function userIndexKey(userId: string, filterParam: string, filterValue: string): Deno.KvKey {
  // Key shape requested: ['users', userId, filterParam]
  // We extend with filterValue for efficient lookups across users.
  return ["users", userId, filterParam, filterValue] as const;
}

function globalIndexKey(filterParam: string, filterValue: string, userId: string): Deno.KvKey {
  // Global index for lookups across all users
  return ["users", "by", filterParam, filterValue, userId] as const;
}

function globalIndexPrefix(filterParam: string, filterValue: string): Deno.KvKey {
  return ["users", "by", filterParam, filterValue] as const;
}

function validateEmail(email: string): boolean {
  const e = email.trim();
  if (!e) return false;
  if (e.length > 320) return false;
  // permissive
  return /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(e);
}

function cleanTags(tags: unknown): string[] {
  if (!Array.isArray(tags)) return [];
  const out: string[] = [];
  for (const t of tags) {
    if (typeof t !== "string") continue;
    const s = normalizeStr(t);
    if (!s) continue;
    if (s.length > 100) continue;
    out.push(s);
  }
  return Array.from(new Set(out)).slice(0, 50);
}

function cleanAttrs(attrs: unknown): Record<string, string> {
  if (!attrs || typeof attrs !== "object" || Array.isArray(attrs)) return {};
  const o = attrs as Record<string, unknown>;
  const out: Record<string, string> = {};
  const entries = Object.entries(o).slice(0, 50);
  for (const [k0, v0] of entries) {
    if (typeof k0 !== "string") continue;
    const k = normalizeStr(k0);
    if (!k || k.length > 50) continue;
    if (typeof v0 !== "string") continue;
    const v = normalizeStr(v0);
    if (!v || v.length > 200) continue;
    out[k] = v;
  }
  return out;
}

async function collectUser(userId: string): Promise<User | null> {
  const res = await kv.get<User>(userKey(userId));
  return res.value ?? null;
}

async function putUser(user: User): Promise<void> {
  const key = userKey(user.id);
  const existing = await kv.get<User>(key);
  const prev = existing.value ?? null;

  const createdAt = prev?.createdAt ?? user.createdAt;
  const updatedAt = user.updatedAt;

  const next: User = {
    ...prev,
    ...user,
    createdAt,
    updatedAt,
  };

  const atomic = kv.atomic();
  atomic.set(key, next);

  // Remove old indexes if updating
  if (prev) {
    if (prev.email) {
      atomic.delete(globalIndexKey("email", prev.email, prev.id));
      atomic.delete(userIndexKey(prev.id, "email", prev.email));
    }
    if (prev.name) {
      atomic.delete(globalIndexKey("name", prev.name, prev.id));
      atomic.delete(userIndexKey(prev.id, "name", prev.name));
    }
    if (Array.isArray(prev.tags)) {
      for (const t of prev.tags) {
        atomic.delete(globalIndexKey("tag", t, prev.id));
        atomic.delete(userIndexKey(prev.id, "tag", t));
      }
    }
    if (prev.attrs && typeof prev.attrs === "object") {
      for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(prev.attrs)) {
        const fv = `${k}:${v}`;
        atomic.delete(globalIndexKey("attr", fv, prev.id));
        atomic.delete(userIndexKey(prev.id, "attr", fv));
      }
    }
  }

  // Add new indexes
  if (next.email) {
    atomic.set(globalIndexKey("email", next.email, next.id), { userId: next.id, updatedAt } satisfies UserIndexValue);
    atomic.set(userIndexKey(next.id, "email", next.email), true);
  }
  if (next.name) {
    atomic.set(globalIndexKey("name", next.name, next.id), { userId: next.id, updatedAt } satisfies UserIndexValue);
    atomic.set(userIndexKey(next.id, "name", next.name), true);
  }
  if (Array.isArray(next.tags)) {
    for (const t of next.tags) {
      atomic.set(globalIndexKey("tag", t, next.id), { userId: next.id, updatedAt } satisfies UserIndexValue);
      atomic.set(userIndexKey(next.id, "tag", t), true);
    }
  }
  if (next.attrs && typeof next.attrs === "object") {
    for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(next.attrs)) {
      const fv = `${k}:${v}`;
      atomic.set(globalIndexKey("attr", fv, next.id), { userId: next.id, updatedAt } satisfies UserIndexValue);
      atomic.set(userIndexKey(next.id, "attr", fv), true);
    }
  }

  const ok = await atomic.commit();
  if (!ok.ok) throw new Error("KV atomic commit failed");
}

async function deleteUser(userId: string): Promise<boolean> {
  const existing = await kv.get<User>(userKey(userId));
  const prev = existing.value;
  if (!prev) return false;

  const atomic = kv.atomic();

  // Remove indexes
  if (prev.email) {
    atomic.delete(globalIndexKey("email", prev.email, prev.id));
    atomic.delete(userIndexKey(prev.id, "email", prev.email));
  }
  if (prev.name) {
    atomic.delete(globalIndexKey("name", prev.name, prev.id));
    atomic.delete(userIndexKey(prev.id, "name", prev.name));
  }
  if (Array.isArray(prev.tags)) {
    for (const t of prev.tags) {
      atomic.delete(globalIndexKey("tag", t, prev.id));
      atomic.delete(userIndexKey(prev.id, "tag", t));
    }
  }
  if (prev.attrs && typeof prev.attrs === "object") {
    for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(prev.attrs)) {
      const fv = `${k}:${v}`;
      atomic.delete(globalIndexKey("attr", fv, prev.id));
      atomic.delete(userIndexKey(prev.id, "attr", fv));
    }
  }

  atomic.delete(userKey(prev.id));

  const ok = await atomic.commit();
  return ok.ok;
}

function parseFilters(sp: URLSearchParams): {
  filters: Array<{ type: "email" | "name" | "tag" | "attr"; value: string }>;
  errors: string[];
} {
  const filters: Array<{ type: "email" | "name" | "tag" | "attr"; value: string }> = [];
  const errors: string[] = [];

  const email = sp.get("email");
  if (email != null) {
    const v = normalizeStr(email);
    if (!validateEmail(v)) errors.push("Invalid email");
    else filters.push({ type: "email", value: v });
  }

  const name = sp.get("name");
  if (name != null) {
    const v = normalizeStr(name);
    if (!v) errors.push("Invalid name");
    else if (v.length > 200) errors.push("Name too long");
    else filters.push({ type: "name", value: v });
  }

  const tag = sp.get("tag");
  if (tag != null) {
    const v = normalizeStr(tag);
    if (!v) errors.push("Invalid tag");
    else if (v.length > 100) errors.push("Tag too long");
    else filters.push({ type: "tag", value: v });
  }

  const attrKey = sp.get("attrKey");
  const attrVal = sp.get("attrVal");
  if (attrKey != null || attrVal != null) {
    const k = normalizeStr(attrKey ?? "");
    const v = normalizeStr(attrVal ?? "");
    if (!k || !v) errors.push("attrKey and attrVal are required together");
    else if (k.length > 50) errors.push("attrKey too long");
    else if (v.length > 200) errors.push("attrVal too long");
    else filters.push({ type: "attr", value: `${k}:${v}` });
  }

  // reject unknown filters if provided via filter=...
  const filterParams = sp.getAll("filter");
  for (const fp of filterParams) {
    const f = normalizeStr(fp);
    if (!f) continue;
    if (!ALLOWED_FILTERS.has(f)) errors.push(`Unsupported filter: ${f}`);
  }

  return { filters, errors };
}

async function searchUsers(reqUrl: URL): Promise<Response> {
  const sp = reqUrl.searchParams;
  const { filters, errors } = parseFilters(sp);
  if (errors.length) return badRequest("Invalid filters", errors);

  const limit = parseLimit(sp);
  const cursorEnc = parseCursor(sp);
  const cursorKey = cursorEnc ? decodeCursor(cursorEnc) : null;

  if (filters.length === 0) {
    // list all users (scan)
    const iter = kv.list<User>({ prefix: ["users"] }, { limit, cursor: cursorKey ?? undefined });
    const users: User[] = [];
    let lastKey: Deno.KvKey | null = null;

    for await (const entry of iter) {
      if (entry.key.length !== 2) continue; // skip indexes
      users.push(entry.value);
      lastKey = entry.key;
    }

    const nextCursor = (iter as unknown as { cursor?: Deno.KvKey }).cursor ?? null;
    return json({
      items: users,
      nextCursor: nextCursor ? encodeCursor(nextCursor) : null,
      usedIndex: null,
    });
  }

  // Use the most selective single index: pick first filter (caller can control) or implement heuristic.
  const primary = filters[0];

  const iter = kv.list<UserIndexValue>(
    { prefix: globalIndexPrefix(primary.type, primary.value) },
    { limit, cursor: cursorKey ?? undefined },
  );

  const hits: UserIndexValue[] = [];
  const candidateIds: string[] = [];
  for await (const entry of iter) {
    const v = entry.value;
    if (!v?.userId) continue;
    hits.push(v);
    candidateIds.push(v.userId);
  }

  const nextCursor = (iter as unknown as { cursor?: Deno.KvKey }).cursor ?? null;

  if (candidateIds.length === 0) {
    return json({
      items: [],
      nextCursor: nextCursor ? encodeCursor(nextCursor) : null,
      usedIndex: { type: primary.type, value: primary.value },
    });
  }

  // Load candidates in batches
  const keys = candidateIds.map((id) => userKey(id));
  const usersRes = await kv.getMany<User>(keys);

  let users = usersRes.map((r) => r.value).filter((u): u is User => !!u);

  // Apply remaining filters in-memory, plus optionally verify primary membership by user-specific index key.
  if (filters.length > 1) {
    const rest = filters.slice(1);
    users = users.filter((u) => {
      for (const f of rest) {
        if (f.type === "email") {
          if (u.email !== f.value) return false;
        } else if (f.type === "name") {
          if (u.name !== f.value) return false;
        } else if (f.type === "tag") {
          if (!Array.isArray(u.tags) || !u.tags.includes(f.value)) return false;
        } else if (f.type === "attr") {
          const [k, v] = f.value.split(":");
          if (!u.attrs || u.attrs[k] !== v) return false;
        }
      }
      return true;
    });
  }

  // Optional: verify primary via user-specific dynamic key pattern ['users', userId, filterParam, filterValue]
  // This demonstrates dynamic key construction from user input for efficient per-user membership checks.
  const verify = sp.get("verify") === "1";
  if (verify) {
    const checks = await kv.getMany<boolean>(
      users.map((u) => userIndexKey(u.id, primary.type, primary.value)),
    );
    const keep = new Set<string>();
    for (let i = 0; i < users.length; i++) {
      if (checks[i]?.value) keep.add(users[i].id);
    }
    users = users.filter((u) => keep.has(u.id));
  }

  return json({
    items: users,
    nextCursor: nextCursor ? encodeCursor(nextCursor) : null,
    usedIndex: { type: primary.type, value: primary.value },
  });
}

async function handleCreateUser(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  let body: any;
  try {
    body = await req.json();
  } catch {
    return badRequest("Expected JSON body");
  }

  const idRaw = typeof body?.id === "string" ? body.id : crypto.randomUUID();
  const id = assertUserId(idRaw);
  if (!id) return badRequest("Invalid id");

  const name = typeof body?.name === "string" ? normalizeStr(body.name) : undefined;
  const email = typeof body?.email === "string" ? normalizeStr(body.email) : undefined;
  const tags = cleanTags(body?.tags);
  const attrs = cleanAttrs(body?.attrs);

  if (email && !validateEmail(email)) return badRequest("Invalid email");
  if (name && name.length > 200) return badRequest("Name too long");

  const t = nowMs();
  const user: User = {
    id,
    createdAt: t,
    updatedAt: t,
    name: name || undefined,
    email: email || undefined,
    tags: tags.length ? tags : undefined,
    attrs: Object.keys(attrs).length ? attrs : undefined,
  };

  await putUser(user);
  return json({ ok: true, user }, { status: 201 });
}

async function handleGetUser(userId: string): Promise<Response> {
  const id = assertUserId(userId);
  if (!id) return badRequest("Invalid userId");

  const user = await collectUser(id);
  if (!user) return notFound("User not found");
  return json({ ok: true, user });
}

async function handlePatchUser(req: Request, userId: string): Promise<Response> {
  const id = assertUserId(userId);
  if (!id) return badRequest("Invalid userId");

  const existing = await collectUser(id);
  if (!existing) return notFound("User not found");

  let body: any;
  try {
    body = await req.json();
  } catch {
    return badRequest("Expected JSON body");
  }

  const name = typeof body?.name === "string" ? normalizeStr(body.name) : undefined;
  const email = typeof body?.email === "string" ? normalizeStr(body.email) : undefined;
  const tags = body?.tags !== undefined ? cleanTags(body.tags) : undefined;
  const attrs = body?.attrs !== undefined ? cleanAttrs(body.attrs) : undefined;

  if (email !== undefined && email !== "" && !validateEmail(email)) return badRequest("Invalid email");
  if (name !== undefined && name !== "" && name.length > 200) return badRequest("Name too long");

  const updated: User = {
    ...existing,
    name: name === undefined ? existing.name : (name || undefined),
    email: email === undefined ? existing.email : (email || undefined),
    tags: tags === undefined ? existing.tags : (tags.length ? tags : undefined),
    attrs: attrs === undefined ? existing.attrs : (Object.keys(attrs).length ? attrs : undefined),
    updatedAt: nowMs(),
  };

  await putUser(updated);
  return json({ ok: true, user: updated });
}

async function handleDeleteUser(userId: string): Promise<Response> {
  const id = assertUserId(userId);
  if (!id) return badRequest("Invalid userId");

  const ok = await deleteUser(id);
  if (!ok) return notFound("User not found");
  return json({ ok: true });
}

function route(req: Request): Promise<Response> | Response {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const pathname = url.pathname.replace(/\/+$/, "") || "/";

  // Basic CORS
  if (req.method === "OPTIONS") {
    return new Response(null, {
      status: 204,
      headers: corsHeaders(req),
    });
  }

  const withCors = async (p: Promise<Response> | Response): Promise<Response> => {
    const res = await p;
    const headers = new Headers(res.headers);
    for (const [k, v] of corsHeaders(req)) headers.set(k, v);
    return new Response(res.body, { status: res.status, headers });
  };

  if (pathname === "/health") {
    return withCors(json({ ok: true, service: "edge-kv-api" }));
  }

  if (pathname === "/users" && req.method === "POST") return withCors(handleCreateUser(req));
  if (pathname === "/users" && req.method === "GET") return withCors(searchUsers(url));

  const m = pathname.match(/^\/users\/([^/]+)$/);
  if (m) {
    const userId = decodeURIComponent(m[1]);
    if (req.method === "GET") return withCors(handleGetUser(userId));
    if (req.method === "PATCH") return withCors(handlePatchUser(req, userId));
    if (req.method === "DELETE") return withCors(handleDeleteUser(userId));
  }

  return withCors(json({ error: "not_found", message: "Route not found" }, { status: 404 }));
}

function corsHeaders(req: Request): Headers {
  const origin = req.headers.get("origin") ?? "*";
  const h = new Headers();
  h.set("access-control-allow-origin", origin);
  h.set("vary", "origin");
  h.set("access-control-allow-methods", "GET,POST,PATCH,DELETE,OPTIONS");
  h.set("access-control-allow-headers", req.headers.get("access-control-request-headers") ?? "content-type,authorization");
  h.set("access-control-max-age", "86400");
  return h;
}

serve((req) => route(req));
